title: The Lost City of Dwarka — Remotion PoC

scenes.text: Beneath the Arabian Sea lies a mystery that has captivated archaeologists for decades.
The legendary city of Dwarka, mentioned in ancient Hindu texts as the kingdom of Lord Krishna.
scenes.generation_prompt: Ancient golden city with ornate temples emerging from mist, cinematic wide shot

scenes.text: For centuries, scholars dismissed Dwarka as pure mythology. Until 2001.
Marine archaeologists from India's National Institute of Ocean Technology made a stunning discovery.
Using side-scan sonar, they found massive stone structures on the ocean floor.
scenes.generation_prompt: Side-scan sonar revealing ancient stone walls on ocean floor, blue-green color palette

scenes.text: Walls, pillars, and what appeared to be a planned urban grid — exactly matching the ancient descriptions.
Carbon dating of artifacts recovered from the site produced dates going back 9,500 years.
If confirmed, this would make Dwarka one of the oldest known urban settlements on Earth.
scenes.generation_prompt: Timeline visualization showing Dwarka at 7500 BCE alongside other ancient civilizations

scenes.text: Older than Mesopotamia. Older than the Egyptian pyramids. A civilization lost to rising seas.
Today, the Indian government has designated the underwater site as a protected national monument.
But the full story of Dwarka remains locked beneath the waves, waiting to be told.
scenes.generation_prompt: Sunlight beams penetrating ocean water illuminating ancient stone ruins, ethereal mood

scenes.text: Subscribe for more stories of lost civilizations hidden in plain sight.
